text:
Market today: Tech is bouncing back after a global AI/chip selloff rattled names like yours. The rebound looks real but fragile — sentiment-driven, not fundamental. Chip and AI stocks are hypersensitive right now, and you're 90% in that lane on margin.

Your holdings: AVGO (-12.89%, your largest position) is your biggest risk — a big name down double digits while flagged TRIM is a margin account's worst combo, size it down. PLTR (-10.31%, SELL signal) is bleeding and has no catalyst shown here to reverse — your stop at $85 gives room but the signal says act. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — margin amplifies any gap-down hard, keep it small and manage it manually as noted.

Watch: 1) Does today's tech rebound hold into the close or fade — a fade would be a warning shot for your chip-heavy book. 2) SNOW is sitting on a +48% gain with a stop at $208 — make sure that stop is live and tight.

Bottom line: Trim AVGO and exit or reduce PLTR before margin turns a rough patch into a real hole.
